wiki.summary: "Rock Your Body" is the third single from Justin Timberlake's solo debut album Justified. The song features vocals in the chorus from Vanessa Marquez, she was at that time signed to Star Trak Entertainment. Released on March 17, 2003, the instrumental, like many from the Justified album were intended for Michael Jackson's 2001 album Invincible & contain influences from both Michael Jackson & Stevie Wonder, Michael ended up passing on the beats, claiming they were too generic for the time. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Rock Your Body" is the third single from Justin Timberlake's solo debut album Justified. The song features vocals in the chorus from Vanessa Marquez, she was at that time signed to Star Trak Entertainment. Released on March 17, 2003, the instrumental, like many from the Justified album were intended for Michael Jackson's 2001 album Invincible & contain influences from both Michael Jackson & Stevie Wonder, Michael ended up passing on the beats, claiming they were too generic for the time. The Neptunes ended up selling the track to Timberlake instead. Timberlake performed this song at the Kids' Choice Awards. The song's music video features Timberlake dancing. The female lead dancer in the music video is Staci Flood lip-syncing Marquez's parts of the song. The video version of the song also features another beatbox breakdown in the middle of the song, much like the one performed in the *NSync single Pop. While Timberlake was performing this song at the Super Bowl XXXVIII halftime show with R&B singer Janet Jackson, at the moment he sang the lyric, "I'll have you naked by the end of this song," he ripped off part of Jackson's costume by accident, exposing her right breast on live television. Timberlake distanced himself from the controversy while Jackson faced much criticism. Timberlake later commented that "America's harsher on women...and unfairly harsh on ethnic people." Rock Your Body debuted on the US Billboard Hot 100 at #61 on the chart week of March 22, 2003, earning the Hot Shot Debut honor. The following week, the song entered the top 40 at #37, it later steadily climbed up the chart until it reached the top 10 at #10, it jumped to its peak of #5 the following week in May 2003, his second top 10 as a lead artist. The song topped the US Mainstream Top 40 chart, where it remained for a week. The song would later be sampled by Don Toliver on his 2026 song "Body". Read more on Last.fm.
